questions.stem: A startup wants to build AI agents quickly without installing any local development tools. Which Agent Platform component should they use to define, test, and deploy agents entirely from a web browser?

options.text: SearchAI -- the knowledge base and RAG pipeline

questions.explanation: Studio is the browser-based IDE where you build, test, and manage agents -- no local installation required. Runtime is the execution engine that runs agents behind the scenes, and SearchAI handles knowledge base ingestion and retrieval.

questions.stem: Agent Platform compiles agent definitions written in the ABL DSL into an ________ (two words, abbreviated IR) that the Runtime executes.
questions.explanation: The ABL compiler transforms your .abl source files into an Intermediate Representation (IR) -- a framework-agnostic JSON structure that the Runtime loads and executes. You never need to work with the IR directly.

questions.stem: In the Quickstart tutorial, what is the purpose of the SUPERVISOR definition that includes HANDOFF rules?

options.text: It compiles agent definitions into executable artifacts for deployment
It analyzes each incoming message and routes it to the appropriate specialist agent
It manages the knowledge base and performs semantic search over documents

questions.explanation: A Supervisor analyzes each incoming message against its HANDOFF rules and routes the conversation to the appropriate specialist agent. It acts as a router -- it does not handle domain tasks directly, compile code, or manage knowledge bases.

questions.stem: When an end user sends a message through WhatsApp to an Agent Platform agent, which component receives the message, loads the agent definition, and manages the conversation session?
questions.explanation: Runtime is the agent execution engine. It receives messages from end users via any channel (chat, voice, SDK, WhatsApp, etc.), loads the compiled agent definition (IR), executes reasoning or step-based flows, manages sessions, and delivers responses. Studio is for development, and SearchAI handles knowledge retrieval.

questions.stem: Agent Platform describes itself as 'DSL-driven agent development.' What is the primary advantage of using a declarative DSL rather than writing imperative code to define agents?

options.text: It requires learning a general-purpose programming language like Python before you can build agents
You describe what your agents should do in a structured, human-readable format, and the platform handles execution, routing, and observability
It eliminates the need for testing agents before deploying them to production

questions.explanation: ABL's declarative approach means you describe the agent's goal, tools, and behavior -- the platform handles how to execute it. This replaces hundreds of lines of framework boilerplate with concise, version-controlled, diffable definitions readable by non-engineers. Testing is still essential before production deployment.
